topic: An employee is transferred to a different department
raw_output:
[story 1]

The memo felt like a physical blow, announcing my relocation to the marketing division. I clutched the glossy paper, my knuckles white, and watched Sarah across the cubicle farm as she laughed at something Mark said. Every time she turned, the light caught the curve of her neck, and a strange warmth bloomed low in my stomach, making my palms sweat. I spent the rest of the afternoon meticulously organizing my desk, a distraction tactic, while my gaze kept drifting. When I finally managed to gather my things, the air felt thick, charged with an unspoken tension that only seemed to center around her presence.

[story 2]

"So, the transfer is official," my manager announced, his tone brisk and professional. I nodded, trying to keep my expression neutral, but my focus was entirely on Elias. He was already moving toward the elevator, his silhouette sharp against the fluorescent lights. I found myself straightening my tie repeatedly, a nervous habit, while cataloging the way his hair fell just so when he turned his head. A flutter started in my chest, a quick, uneven rhythm that made breathing shallow. I rehearsed a casual greeting in my head, but the words dissolved into a tangle of wanting to say something profound, something that would bridge the distance between us.

[story 3]

Walking into the new wing of the building felt like stepping onto a different planet. The new department buzzed with a different kind of energy, and amidst the chatter, I spotted her instantly. She was sketching in a notebook near the window, sunlight illuminating the delicate line of her jaw. My internal monologue stuttered. I felt an urgent need to approach, to simply stand near her, to absorb her presence. When I finally managed to cross the room, my voice came out a little breathy. "Excuse me," I managed, the simple request sounding monumental, as I fought the sudden, overwhelming urge to just stare until she looked up.
